texto: The truth is that our enjoyments and our evaluations, like our trades, are learned; intensive knowledge, as well as extensive, is acquired. We learn how to value possessions as well as how to make them; our passions, our disgusts, and our ambitions are learned. Just as we have evolved ways of transmuting physical elements from one to another, so we have evolved ways of transmuting experience into meaning.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Denham Sutcliffe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The truth is that our enjoyments and our evaluations, like our trades, are learned; intensive kno…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, formation, humanity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humanity e meaning.

We learn how to value possessions as well as how to make them; our passions, our disgusts, and our ambitions are learned. Just as we have evolved ways of transmuting physical elements from one to another, so we have evolved ways of transmuting experience into meaning.

texto: Do you think that romantic love is natural; that the life of trade is honorable; that freedom of worship is desirable? Very likely you think none of those things; you have uncritically absorbed them, and only since you came to college begun to learn that they are al comparatively recent and local notions, the products of past conflicts and choices. As the hand of the dyer is tinged by what it works in, so your tastes, ambitions, and values take their quality from a context that was created for you before you were born.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Denham Sutcliffe que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Do you think that romantic love is natural; that the life of trade is honorable; that freedom of …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, history, knowledge, com foco em trabalho e knowledge.

texto: But it seems to me inevitable that any person who gives thoughtful and imaginative attention to literature must be awakened in his sensibilities, enlarged in his sympathies, sharpened in his critical faculties.

texto: What, to many, passes for thought, is usually a compound of prejudice, desire, and whim.
explicacao: Citação de Denham Sutcliffe sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “What, to many, passes for thought, is usually a compound of prejudice, desire, and whim.” articula reflexao, prejudice, thought em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como thought.

texto: Our professional competence and pride should rest not alone in our possession of knowledge but as well in our ability to communicate it. Of course we shall carry on our research, and of course we shall applaud the colleague who 'produces,' but we shan't be happy if he offers that as a substitute for inspiring young people with a desire for knowledge, a sense of taste, and a regard for virtue.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Denham Sutcliffe sobre temas ligados a motivacao. A afirmação “Our professional competence and pride should rest not alone in our possession of knowledge but as…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, teachers, teaching, útil em momentos de inspiracao e teaching.

Of course we shall carry on our research, and of course we shall applaud the colleague who 'produces,' but we shan't be happy if he offers that as a substitute for inspiring young people with a desire for knowledge, a sense of taste, and a regard for virtue.
